---
title: "Sample size: diagnostic test (sensitivity & specificity)"
date: "2026-05-08"
categories: ["sample size", "diagnostic", "sensitivity", "specificity"]
description: "Sample-size calculator for a diagnostic-accuracy study (Buderer 1996). Computes the number of diseased and non-diseased participants needed to estimate sensitivity and specificity with a target precision."
pagetitle: "Sample size for a diagnostic-accuracy study — StudyStats"
output-file: diagnostic.html
aliases:
  - shiny_sample_size_diagnostics_sensitivity_specificity.html
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    css:
      - _calculator.css
resources:
  - _calculator.js
---

```{=html}
<section class="ss-calc" id="diagCalc" aria-labelledby="diagTitle">
  <h2 id="diagTitle">Sample size for a diagnostic-accuracy study</h2>
  <p class="ss-lede">
    Use this tool when you're evaluating a <strong>new test</strong> against a
    reference standard and you need to estimate <em>sensitivity</em> and
    <em>specificity</em> with a target precision. Based on Buderer (1996).
  </p>

  <div class="ss-quickstart" role="group" aria-label="Quick start scenarios">
    <button type="button" data-preset="mmed" class="active">▶ MMed default</button>
    <button type="button" data-preset="screening">▶ Screening test</button>
    <button type="button" data-preset="rare">▶ Rare disease</button>
  </div>

  <div class="ss-grid">
    <div class="ss-inputs">
      <div class="ss-field">
        <label for="sens">Expected sensitivity <span class="ss-symbol">Sn</span></label>
        <p class="ss-tip">Probability the new test correctly identifies a diseased person.</p>
        <input type="number" id="sens" min="0.5" max="0.999" step="0.01" value="0.85">
        <details class="ss-justify">
          <summary>How to justify this number</summary>
          <div class="ss-justify-body">
            <p>Use a published estimate from a comparable population, or a
            pilot. If you have only a guess, set sensitivity at 0.80 — this
            maximises required sample (most conservative).</p>
          </div>
        </details>
      </div>

      <div class="ss-field">
        <label for="spec">Expected specificity <span class="ss-symbol">Sp</span></label>
        <p class="ss-tip">Probability the new test correctly identifies a non-diseased person.</p>
        <input type="number" id="spec" min="0.5" max="0.999" step="0.01" value="0.90">
      </div>

      <div class="ss-field">
        <label for="w">Margin of error <span class="ss-symbol">w</span></label>
        <p class="ss-tip">Half-width of the 95 % confidence interval around Sn and Sp. 0.05 = ± 5 percentage points.</p>
        <input type="number" id="w" min="0.005" max="0.20" step="0.005" value="0.05">
      </div>

      <div class="ss-field">
        <label for="prev">Disease prevalence in the sampling frame</label>
        <p class="ss-tip">Proportion of patients in your recruitment setting who actually have the disease (by reference standard).</p>
        <input type="number" id="prev" min="0.01" max="0.99" step="0.01" value="0.20">
        <details class="ss-justify">
          <summary>How to justify this number</summary>
          <div class="ss-justify-body">
            <p>Determines how many people you must screen to obtain enough
            diseased / non-diseased participants. If recruitment is by
            <strong>case-control design</strong> (you separately enrol cases and
            controls), prevalence is irrelevant — recruit n_diseased and
            n_healthy directly.</p>
          </div>
        </details>
      </div>

      <div class="ss-field">
        <label for="alpha">Confidence level <span class="ss-symbol">1 − α</span></label>
        <select id="alpha">
          <option value="0.10">90 %</option>
          <option value="0.05" selected>95 % (standard)</option>
          <option value="0.01">99 %</option>
        </select>
      </div>

      <div class="ss-field">
        <label for="dropout">Expected drop-out / indeterminate (%)</label>
        <input type="number" id="dropout" min="0" max="50" step="1" value="10">
      </div>
    </div>

    <div class="ss-result" aria-live="polite">
      <h3>You need</h3>
      <div id="ssBig" class="ss-big">—</div>
      <div id="ssSub" class="ss-sub">—</div>

      <div id="ssInterpret" class="ss-interpret">Adjust the inputs to see your sample size.</div>

      <div class="ss-actions">
        <button type="button" id="copyMethods">📋 Copy methods paragraph</button>
        <button type="button" id="copyR" class="ss-secondary">📋 Copy R code</button>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <details class="ss-details">
    <summary>What does this calculation actually do?</summary>
    <div class="ss-details-body">
      <p>Buderer (1996) treats sensitivity and specificity as proportions
      estimated from disjoint subsets (diseased vs non-diseased):</p>
      <pre>n_diseased = z² · Sn · (1 − Sn) / w²
n_healthy  = z² · Sp · (1 − Sp) / w²
n_total    = max( n_diseased / prev,  n_healthy / (1 − prev) )</pre>
      <p>The <em>total</em> reflects the number of people you must screen to
      naturally accrue enough diseased and non-diseased participants. With a
      <strong>case-control</strong> design (separate streams), recruit
      <code>n_diseased</code> cases and <code>n_healthy</code> controls
      directly — ignore the total.</p>
      <p><strong>References:</strong> Buderer NMF. <em>Acad Emerg Med</em>
      1996;3:895–900. · Hajian-Tilaki K. <em>J Biomed Inform</em> 2014;48:193–204.
      · Bujang MA, Adnan TH. <em>J Clin Diagn Res</em> 2016;10:YE01–YE06.</p>
    </div>
  </details>
</section>

<script src="_calculator.js"></script>
<script>
(function () {
  'use strict';
  var SS = window.StudyStats;

  var presets = {
    mmed:      { sens: 0.85, spec: 0.90, w: 0.05, prev: 0.20, alpha: '0.05', dropout: 10 },
    screening: { sens: 0.95, spec: 0.85, w: 0.03, prev: 0.05, alpha: '0.05', dropout: 10 },
    rare:      { sens: 0.85, spec: 0.95, w: 0.05, prev: 0.02, alpha: '0.05', dropout: 10 }
  };

  var $ = function (id) { return document.getElementById(id); };
  var ids = ['sens','spec','w','prev','alpha','dropout'];

  function readVals() {
    return {
      sens: parseFloat($('sens').value),
      spec: parseFloat($('spec').value),
      w: parseFloat($('w').value),
      prev: parseFloat($('prev').value),
      alpha: parseFloat($('alpha').value),
      dropout: parseFloat($('dropout').value) / 100
    };
  }

  function nProp(p, w, alpha) {
    var z = SS.qnorm(1 - alpha / 2);
    return z * z * p * (1 - p) / (w * w);
  }

  function update() {
    var v = readVals();
    var big = $('ssBig'), sub = $('ssSub'), interp = $('ssInterpret');
    if (!(v.sens > 0 && v.sens < 1) || !(v.spec > 0 && v.spec < 1) ||
        !(v.w > 0) || !(v.prev > 0 && v.prev < 1)) {
      big.textContent = '—'; big.classList.add('ss-warn');
      sub.textContent = 'Set valid inputs.';
      interp.textContent = 'Adjust the inputs to see your sample size.';
      return;
    }
    var nD = nProp(v.sens, v.w, v.alpha);
    var nH = nProp(v.spec, v.w, v.alpha);
    var nTotal = Math.max(nD / v.prev, nH / (1 - v.prev));

    var infl = 1 / Math.max(1e-9, 1 - v.dropout);
    var nDc = Math.ceil(nD * infl);
    var nHc = Math.ceil(nH * infl);
    var nTc = Math.ceil(nTotal * infl);

    big.classList.toggle('ss-warn', nTc > 2000);
    big.textContent = SS.formatN(nTc) + ' to screen';
    sub.textContent = SS.formatN(nDc) + ' diseased  ·  ' +
                      SS.formatN(nHc) + ' non-diseased' +
                      (v.dropout > 0 ? '  ·  +' + (v.dropout * 100).toFixed(0) + '% drop-out' : '');

    interp.textContent = 'To estimate sensitivity (' + (v.sens * 100).toFixed(0) +
      '%) and specificity (' + (v.spec * 100).toFixed(0) + '%) each within ± ' +
      (v.w * 100).toFixed(1) + ' percentage points at ' +
      ((1 - v.alpha) * 100).toFixed(0) + '% confidence, you need to recruit ' +
      SS.formatN(nDc) + ' diseased and ' + SS.formatN(nHc) +
      ' non-diseased participants. With a disease prevalence of ' +
      (v.prev * 100).toFixed(0) + '% in your sampling frame, this means screening ' +
      SS.formatN(nTc) + ' patients.' +
      (nTc > 2000 ? ' ⚠ Consider a case-control recruitment design or relaxing the margin.' : '');
  }

  function applyPreset(name) {
    var p = presets[name]; if (!p) return;
    $('sens').value = p.sens; $('spec').value = p.spec; $('w').value = p.w;
    $('prev').value = p.prev; $('alpha').value = p.alpha; $('dropout').value = p.dropout;
    update();
    document.querySelectorAll('.ss-quickstart button').forEach(function (b) {
      b.classList.toggle('active', b.dataset.preset === name);
    });
  }

  function methodsParagraph() {
    var v = readVals();
    var nD = nProp(v.sens, v.w, v.alpha);
    var nH = nProp(v.spec, v.w, v.alpha);
    var nTotal = Math.max(nD / v.prev, nH / (1 - v.prev));
    var infl = 1 / Math.max(1e-9, 1 - v.dropout);
    var nDc = Math.ceil(nD * infl), nHc = Math.ceil(nH * infl), nTc = Math.ceil(nTotal * infl);
    return 'Sample size for the diagnostic-accuracy study was calculated using the ' +
      'method of Buderer (1996), assuming an expected sensitivity of ' +
      (v.sens * 100).toFixed(0) + '% and specificity of ' + (v.spec * 100).toFixed(0) +
      '%, each estimated to within ± ' + (v.w * 100).toFixed(1) + ' percentage points ' +
      'at the ' + ((1 - v.alpha) * 100).toFixed(0) + '% confidence level. ' +
      'With a disease prevalence of ' + (v.prev * 100).toFixed(0) + '% in the sampling frame ' +
      'and a ' + (v.dropout * 100).toFixed(0) + '% allowance for indeterminate or missing results, ' +
      'this requires recruitment of ' + SS.formatN(nDc) + ' diseased and ' + SS.formatN(nHc) +
      ' non-diseased participants, corresponding to a total screening sample of ' +
      SS.formatN(nTc) + '. ' +
      'Calculations were performed with the StudyStats sample-size tool ' +
      '(https://study-stats.com/tools/diagnostic.html).';
  }

  function rCode() {
    var v = readVals();
    return [
      '# Diagnostic-test sample size (Buderer 1996)',
      'sens  <- ' + v.sens, 'spec  <- ' + v.spec,
      'w     <- ' + v.w + '   # absolute precision',
      'prev  <- ' + v.prev, 'alpha <- ' + v.alpha, 'dropout <- ' + v.dropout,
      'z     <- qnorm(1 - alpha / 2)',
      '',
      'n_diseased <- z^2 * sens * (1 - sens) / w^2',
      'n_healthy  <- z^2 * spec * (1 - spec) / w^2',
      'n_total    <- max(n_diseased / prev, n_healthy / (1 - prev))',
      '',
      'ceiling(n_diseased / (1 - dropout))',
      'ceiling(n_healthy  / (1 - dropout))',
      'ceiling(n_total    / (1 - dropout))'
    ].join('\n');
  }

  ids.forEach(function (id) {
    var el = $(id);
    el.addEventListener('input', update);
    el.addEventListener('change', update);
  });
  document.querySelectorAll('.ss-quickstart button').forEach(function (b) {
    b.addEventListener('click', function () { applyPreset(b.dataset.preset); });
  });
  $('copyMethods').addEventListener('click', function () { SS.copyText(methodsParagraph()); });
  $('copyR').addEventListener('click', function () { SS.copyText(rCode()); });

  update();
})();
</script>
```
